title: POST Request with Body
overview: Send POST, PUT, or PATCH requests with JSON bodies, maps, or POJOs.
whyItMatters: Most state-changing operations in an API require sending data in the request body. Mastering body serialization is essential for testing creation and update endpoints.
explanation: REST Assured supports multiple ways to define a request body: Raw Strings, HashMaps, or POJOs (Plain Old Java Objects). Passing a POJO is the most robust approach, as REST Assured will automatically serialize it to JSON using Jackson or Gson. You must specify the `ContentType.JSON` header so the server knows how to parse the incoming data.
keyPoints: Pass a raw String, a `Map<String, Object>`, or a POJO to `.body()`.
REST Assured automatically serializes Maps and POJOs to JSON.
Use Jackson or Gson annotations on POJOs to control serialization.
codeExample:
// Using a POJO
UserPayload user = new UserPayload("John Doe", "john@example.com");

given().
    baseUri("https://api.example.com").
    contentType(ContentType.JSON).
    body(user).
when().
    post("/users").
then().
    statusCode(201).
    body("message", equalTo("User created"));
realWorldExample: When testing a 'Create Account' endpoint, you populate a Java object with mock user data, pass it to the `.body()` method, and verify that the database successfully registers the user.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to set `contentType(ContentType.JSON)`, resulting in 415 Unsupported Media Type errors.
Using raw Strings for large JSON payloads, which is hard to maintain and prone to syntax errors.
Missing default constructors in POJOs, causing serialization failures.
bestPractices: Prefer POJOs over raw Strings or Maps for better type safety and maintainability.
Use libraries like Lombok to reduce boilerplate code in POJO classes.
Create test data factories to generate POJOs with random/faker data.

interviewQuestions.q: How does REST Assured convert a Java object into a JSON payload?
interviewQuestions.a: It uses a serialization library like Jackson or Gson. If one of these is in the classpath, REST Assured automatically maps the POJO fields to JSON properties.

interviewQuestions.q: What happens if you forget to set the Content-Type header in a POST request?
interviewQuestions.a: The server will likely reject the request with a 415 Unsupported Media Type or a 400 Bad Request, as it doesn't know how to parse the body.

revisionSummary: Send JSON bodies in POST requests using POJOs and contentType(ContentType.JSON).
